ocr.verbatim: PT NAME - AGE/SEX - 58Y/F CHEST PA DATE - 24/03/2026
ocr.english: PT NAME - AGE/SEX - 58Y/F CHEST PA DATE - 24/03/2026

records.summary: A posteroanterior (PA) chest X-ray of a 58-year-old female, dated March 24, 2026. The image shows the bony thorax, lung fields, heart, and diaphragm. The right lung appears more radiolucent than the left. The heart silhouette is visible in the mediastinum.
key_observations: Right lung appears more radiolucent than left
Diaphragm and bony thorax structures visible

records.stage_evidence: This is a radiology report, not a procedural image.

ocr.verbatim:
Patient Name: 
Age / Sex: 58Y/F
Referring Physician:
Study Date: 24/03/2026
DIGITAL RADIOGRAPH CHEST PA VIEW
Report:
Bilateral hila are prominent -likely nodal/vascular.
Prominent of broncho-vascular marking and hila-bronchitis.
Both domes of diaphragm and CP angles are normal.
No evidence of mediastinal shift is seen.
Cardiac size is normal.
Bony cage appear normal.
soft tissue appear normal.
Ad-clinical correlation further evaluation.
M.D. Radiodiagnosis
Reg. No.- 39917
The science of radiology is based upon interpretation of shadows of normal and abnormal tissue. This is neither complete nor accurate; hence, findings should always be interpreted in to the light of clinico-pathological correlation. This is a professional opinion, not a diagnosis. Not meant for medico legal purposes.

records.summary: This is a typed radiology report for a digital chest X-ray (PA view) performed on a 58-year-old female on March 24, 2026. The report notes prominent hila, broncho-vascular markings, and normal cardiac and bony cage appearance, with a recommendation for clinical correlation.
key_observations: Findings include prominent bilateral hila, broncho-vascular markings, and hila-bronchitis.
Cardiac size, bony cage, and soft tissues are normal.
Report recommends further evaluation with clinical correlation.
Report includes a disclaimer that findings are a professional opinion, not a diagnosis, and not meant for medico-legal purposes.
